title: crataegus collina var. sordida
intro: Crataegus collina var. sordida, also known as the rough hawthorn, is a variety of the crataegus collina plant, which belongs to the Rosaceae family and is a type of thorny shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The bark and leaves of the rough hawthorn plant contain flavonoids, triterpenes, and other bioactive compounds, which have been reported to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiotonic effects. The health benefits associated with the use of rough hawthorn include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Rough hawthorn can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
